headline: US Considers Requiring Permits for Nvidia, AMD Global AI Chip Sales
summary: Now, the Trump administration is considering taking a formal role in the industry that would include similarly sweeping powers.  Officials at the US Commerce Department have written draft regulations that would restrict AI chip shipments to anywhere in the world without American approval, giving Washington broad control over whether other countries can build facilities for training and running artificial-intelligence models — and under what conditions.  The proposed rule — which could change substantially or be shelved entirely — would require companies to seek US permission for virtually all exports of AI accelerators from the likes of Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices Inc., a global expansion of curbs that currently cover around 40 countries, according to people familiar with the matter.

content.title: US Considers Requiring Permits for Nvidia, AMD Global AI Chip Sales
content.summary: Now, the Trump administration is considering taking a formal role in the industry that would include similarly sweeping powers.  Officials at the US Commerce Department have written draft regulations that would restrict AI chip shipments to anywhere in the world without American approval, giving Washington broad control over whether other countries can build facilities for training and running artificial-intelligence models — and under what conditions.  The proposed rule — which could change substantially or be shelved entirely — would require companies to seek US permission for virtually all exports of AI accelerators from the likes of Nvidia and Advanced Micro Devices Inc., a global expansion of curbs that currently cover around 40 countries, according to people familiar with the matter.

headline: Taiwan's Foxconn says everyone will feel impact of prolonged Iran conflict
summary: Everyone will feel the impact on prices of oil and raw materials if the U.S. and ‌Israeli conflict with Iran drags on, the chairman of Foxconn, the ‌world's largest electronics maker and Nvidia's key AI server maker, said on Friday.  Speaking to reporters at Foxconn's headquarters in Taipei's neighbouring city of New Taipei, Young Liu said the fallout for his company was currently limited, however, ‌and he expected 2026 ⁠to be a very good year for it.  Given the region's key role as a global oil producer, if the war drags on it could push oil to $100 a ​barrel, and ​push up prices for raw materials, ​Liu added.

content.title: Taiwan's Foxconn says everyone will feel impact of prolonged Iran conflict
content.summary: Everyone will feel the impact on prices of oil and raw materials if the U.S. and ‌Israeli conflict with Iran drags on, the chairman of Foxconn, the ‌world's largest electronics maker and Nvidia's key AI server maker, said on Friday. headline: Broadcom well placed for 'generational' AI investment, CIO says
summary:
STORY: Broadcom shares jumped on Thursday after the company predicted over $100 billion in AI chip sales next year, signaling rapid share gains in the market dominated by Nvidia.

As demand for AI training, inference and automated “agent” systems grows, Ahlsten says companies are pouring billions into specialized chips, calling it "the largest generational investment that I've seen in my career."

"Clearly with Hock Tan, their CEO talking about $100 billion in sales for custom silicon, fantastic opportunities there and really a big driver of Broadcom and we think this is quite durable as we go from training A.I. models to the search engine world," he added.

content.title: Broadcom well placed for 'generational' AI investment, CIO says
content.summary:
STORY: Broadcom shares jumped on Thursday after the company predicted over $100 billion in AI chip sales next year, signaling rapid share gains in the market dominated by Nvidia.

As demand for AI training, inference and automated “agent” systems grows, Ahlsten says companies are pouring billions into specialized chips, calling it "the largest generational investment that I've seen in my career."

"Clearly with Hock Tan, their CEO talking about $100 billion in sales for custom silicon, fantastic opportunities there and really a big driver of Broadcom and we think this is quite durable as we go from training A.I. models to the search engine world," he added.
